title: ononis alba var. trifoliata
intro: Ononis alba var. trifoliata, also known as the White Restharrow or White Bindweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family Fabaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Ononis alba var. trifoliata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
